bio.p1: I'm Eric - a self-taught engineer and the person behind every project here. I learned to build by shipping, and solving real problems: late nights, broken side projects, and pushing things until they worked. No classroom, just a stubborn habit of figuring it out.
bio.p2: Today I help small and mid-sized businesses turn slow, expensive problems into software that quietly pays for itself. Most of it is boring on purpose: automating the repetitive tasks that cost people hours, building custom tools that replace fragile spreadsheets, and fixing websites so they actually bring in revenue. I work as a long-term partner, not a vendor who vanishes after launch - two of my client relationships have run past four years.
bio.p3: The results are what I care about. For one client I grew revenue 270% by rebuilding their SEO and bringing in the right traffic. As the first technical hire at Friends in Flats I built the platform from scratch to 20,000+ users and led a team of four. And I build my own products to stay sharp - PromptSloth reached 7,000+ users and $3K in just 9 months on organic search alone, with no active marketing, and now runs on autopilot. Clarity over noise, outcomes over buzzwords.

principles.title: Beyond the comfort zone
principles.copy: I do my best work on the problems that push me. If it's a little hard, it's interesting.

principles.title: Deep focus
principles.copy: When I'm building, three hours feel like twenty minutes. That's when the good work happens.

principles.title: Minimal by default
principles.copy: Clear mind, clean setup, no bloat. I ship what moves your numbers and nothing you don't need.

cta.title: Have something worth building?
cta.subtitle: Let's find the software that pays for itself.

meta.title: About Eric Strohmaier - Custom Software Developer
meta.description: Eric Strohmaier is a self-taught software engineer who builds custom software, internal tools, automations, and high-converting websites that make small and mid-sized businesses more money.
meta.ogDescription: Self-taught engineer building software that pays for itself for small and mid-sized businesses.
